notes: Vendored Rust checks inline enum value size. Crystal models the AST as reference objects under a different runtime/object layout, so Rust's `size_of::<Ast>()` heuristic is not a meaningful parity target here.

Vendored Rust checks a custom `Drop` implementation for recursive AST values. Crystal uses GC-managed reference objects and does not expose an equivalent user-defined destructor path for this AST surface.
matches current ASCII class parse/backtrack coverage
representative alternation parsing covered for flat and grouped cases; local AST still coalesces adjacent verbatim literals compared with upstream literal-by-literal AST shape
named capture success, duplicate rejection, invalid syntax, and structured capture-name EOF diagnostics covered

AstParser now exposes parse_with_comments and captures Rust-style comment text/spans, but local AST still coalesces adjacent verbatim literals instead of matching upstream literal-by-literal AST shape

representative exact, at-least, bounded, nongreedy, invalid descending-range, decimal overflow, unclosed counts, invalid ranges, duplicate-capture auxiliary-span, special-word-boundary ambiguity, and Unicode-property EOF structured error cases covered; the full upstream matrix is still larger.

decimal parsing is now covered indirectly through counted repetition for representative success, empty, leading-zero, overflow, and Rust-style DecimalInvalid span propagation; the direct internal helper is still not exposed separately in Crystal.

representative meta/special escapes, perl-class escape parsing, fixed/braced hex metadata, special-word-boundary ambiguity and unrecognized cases, and structured EOF/invalid-hex diagnostics covered; full upstream escape matrix still missing.

individual flag acceptance is covered through scoped/public group parsing for i,m,s,U,u,R,x, and duplicate/repeated-negation/dangling-negation auxiliary-span diagnostics are asserted directly; direct internal parse_flag helper parity is still missing.

global flags, scoped flags, negation placement, duplicate flags, repeated negation, dangling negation, unexpected EOF, and unrecognized flag cases covered with structured error-kind/span assertions for the duplicate/negation cases.

captures, named captures, non-captures, capture-name EOF diagnostics, unsupported look-ahead, duplicate-capture auxiliary spans, and Unicode-property EOF diagnostics are covered; richer remaining parser error parity still missing.
positive x/u/U brace forms and representative error cases covered, including direct AST assertions for preserved brace-form metadata and original escape prefix.
representative positive long fixed-width form and error cases covered, including direct AST assertions for preserved 8-digit form metadata and `\U` prefix.
representative positive short Unicode fixed-width form and error cases covered, including direct AST assertions for preserved 4-digit form metadata and `\u` prefix.
representative positive byte fixed-width form and error cases covered, including direct AST assertions for preserved 2-digit form metadata and `\x` prefix.

public parser coverage now includes literal `]` and the escaped metacharacter matrix from the Rust holistic test; full AST span/kind parity still differs because verbatim literal runs are coalesced locally
verbose-mode parsing, whitespace toggles, grouped whitespace, escaped-space handling, and hex spacing covered
nest_limit enforced through public parser
public parser handling of literal newlines between tokens covered; full upstream line/column span parity is blocked on the simplified Position/Span model
octal option and representative parse/parse_escape behavior covered
representative \\d \\D \\s \\w parse coverage now includes direct AST concat coverage for perl-class-followed-by-literal cases like `\\dz`; the full direct parse_escape matrix is still larger.
public parser coverage exists for dot, anchors, and representative verbatim literals; internal parse_primitive-only cases like bare | are not directly exposed in Crystal

public parser coverage now includes ASCII/unicode/perl items, binary ops, leading `]`/`-` opening cases, escaped `[` and `]`, invalid class escapes, descending ranges, and verbose-mode comment regression handling; full upstream AST-shape/span parity is still missing

public parser coverage exercises the Rust opening-edge behavior for leading `]`, leading `-`, negated leading `]`, escaped `[`, and the verbose-mode `(?x)[-#]` regression; the internal helper itself is not exposed in Crystal
representative greedy, nongreedy, alternation-edge, and repetition-missing cases covered; full upstream error-kind/span parity still missing
